project.name: SignTutor
project.description: A browser-based, vision-driven sign language learning platform with on-device inference.

nodes.name: adapt.md
nodes.summary: Adapt an existing design to a different context: screen size, device, platform, or use case — rethinking the experience for the new context, not scaling pixels

nodes.name: animate.md
nodes.summary: Add motion that conveys state, gives feedback, and clarifies hierarchy; covers entrance animations, micro-interactions, state transitions, technical implementation, and accessibility

nodes.name: audit.md
nodes.summary: Run systematic technical quality checks across accessibility, performance, theming, responsive design, and anti-patterns; generate a scored audit report

nodes.name: bolder.md
nodes.summary: Increase visual impact and personality through stronger hierarchy, committed scale, decisive type, and intentional drama — rejecting generic AI 'bold' defaults

nodes.name: brand.md
nodes.summary: Brand register for design surfaces where design IS the product: typography selection procedure, reflex-reject lists, color strategies, layout, imagery, and motion for brand surfaces

nodes.name: clarify.md
nodes.summary: Find unclear, confusing, or poorly written interface text and rewrite it with specificity, clarity, and appropriate tone across errors, forms, CTAs, empty states, and more

nodes.name: codex.md
nodes.summary: Visual direction and asset production for craft flow when native image generation is available: palette generation, mock exploration, approval loop, and asset slicing

nodes.name: cognitive-load.md
nodes.summary: Assess and reduce cognitive load in interfaces: intrinsic, extraneous, and germane load types with checklist and working memory rules

nodes.name: color-and-contrast.md
nodes.summary: Color spaces (OKLCH), building functional palettes, WCAG contrast requirements, dangerous color combinations, light and dark mode theming, and design token hierarchy

nodes.name: colorize.md
nodes.summary: Replace timid grayscale or single-accent designs with a strategic palette: color strategies (Restrained/Committed/Full palette/Drenched) and systematic application

nodes.name: craft.md
nodes.summary: Build features with impeccable UX and UI quality: shape the design, load references, visual direction, build to production quality, iterate visually, and present

nodes.name: critique.md
nodes.summary: Design critique assessment: two independent sub-agent assessments, heuristic scoring, anti-pattern detection, persona red flags, snapshot persistence, and recommended actions

nodes.name: delight.md
nodes.summary: Find moments where personality and unexpected polish turn a functional interface into one users remember: micro-interactions, copy personality, illustrations, easter eggs

nodes.name: document.md
nodes.summary: Generate DESIGN.md capturing the current visual design system following the Google Stitch format: YAML frontmatter with design tokens and six-section markdown body

nodes.name: extract.md
nodes.summary: Identify reusable patterns, components, and design tokens, then extract and consolidate them into the design system for systematic reuse

nodes.name: harden.md
nodes.summary: Harden interfaces against edge cases: text overflow, internationalization, error handling, edge cases, input validation, accessibility resilience, and performance resilience

nodes.name: heuristics-scoring.md
nodes.summary: Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics scoring guide with detailed criteria per heuristic (0-4 scale), score summary bands, and issue severity definitions (P0-P3)

nodes.name: interaction-design.md
nodes.summary: Interaction design patterns: eight interactive states, focus rings, form design, loading states, modals, popover API, dropdown positioning, keyboard navigation, and gestures

nodes.name: live.md
nodes.summary: Interactive live variant mode: select elements in browser, generate three AI-driven HTML+CSS variants with hot-swap via HMR, parameter controls, accept/discard workflow

nodes.name: motion-design.md
nodes.summary: Motion design guidelines: duration rules, easing curves, premium motion materials, staggered animations, reduced motion requirements, and perceived performance

nodes.name: Impeccable Skill Reference
nodes.summary: Reference documents for the Impeccable skill — design system covering adapt, animate, audit, bolder, brand, clarify, codex, cognitive-load, color, craft, critique, delight, document, extract, harden, heuristics, interaction, live, and motion design

nodes.name: onboard.md
nodes.summary: Guide for designing user onboarding experiences — getting users to first value as fast as possible through welcome screens, empty states, tooltips, guided tours, and interactive tutorials.

nodes.name: optimize.md
nodes.summary: Performance optimization reference covering loading, rendering, animation, React/framework optimization, network optimization, and Core Web Vitals — measure first, then fix the actual bottleneck.

nodes.name: overdrive.md
nodes.summary: Push interfaces past conventional limits with WebGL, View Transitions API, scroll-driven animations, spring physics, and other advanced browser techniques — with progressive enhancement discipline.

nodes.name: personas.md
nodes.summary: Five user archetypes (Alex, Jordan, Sam, Riley, Casey) for persona-based design testing — each exposes different failure modes and red flags that a single perspective would miss.

nodes.name: polish.md
nodes.summary: Final polish pass reference — design system alignment, visual alignment, typography refinement, interaction states, micro-interactions, edge cases, and comprehensive quality checklist.

nodes.name: product.md
nodes.summary: Product register reference for app UIs — typography, color, layout, components, motion conventions, and what product interfaces should avoid or permit.

nodes.name: quieter.md
nodes.summary: Reduce visual intensity in loud or overstimulating designs — color refinement, visual weight reduction, simplification, motion reduction, and composition refinement without losing personality.

nodes.name: responsive-design.md
nodes.summary: Responsive design reference covering mobile-first CSS, content-driven breakpoints, input method detection, safe areas, responsive images with srcset/picture, and layout adaptation patterns.

nodes.name: shape.md
nodes.summary: Design shaping workflow — structured discovery interview, visual direction probes, and design brief production before any code is written. Produces a confirmed brief that guides implementation.

nodes.name: spatial-design.md
nodes.summary: Spatial design reference — 4pt spacing systems, semantic tokens, self-adjusting grids, visual hierarchy through multiple dimensions, container queries, optical adjustments, and elevation.

nodes.name: teach.md
nodes.summary: Teach flow — gathers design context and writes PRODUCT.md (strategic) and DESIGN.md (visual) files through codebase exploration, strategic questioning, and structured document generation.

nodes.name: typeset.md
nodes.summary: Typesetting improvement workflow — font selection, hierarchy establishment, readability fixes, weight consistency, and modular type scales for both brand and product registers.

nodes.name: typography.md
nodes.summary: Deep typography reference — vertical rhythm, modular scales, readability, font pairing principles, web font loading strategies, fluid vs fixed type, OpenType features, and accessibility.

nodes.name: ux-writing.md
nodes.summary: UX writing reference — button labels, error messages, empty states, voice vs tone, accessibility, translation considerations, terminology consistency, and confirmation dialog best practices.

nodes.name: BUILD_PROMPT.md
nodes.summary: Build prompt for SignTutor v1 — production-grade Next.js sign language learning platform with on-device ML inference. Covers tech stack (Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind, onnxruntime-web, MediaPipe Hands), hard requirements (on-device privacy, WCAG 2.2 AA, confidence-gated verdict, per-finger panel, JS↔Python parity), v1 page routes, 8 architectural decisions to preserve, acceptance criteria, and stretch goals.

nodes.name: BUILD_PROMPT_ML_EXTENSION.md
nodes.summary: Build prompt for SignTutor v2 ML extension — replaces synthetic training data with real-world datasets, adds J/Z motion letters, scales dynamic vocabulary from 8 to 200 signs, adds non-manual marker scoring via MediaPipe Holistic, on-device personalization via low-rank adapter, confidence calibration with temperature scaling, multi-language beta support (BSL/ArSL), and continuous SLR research preview. Defines 8 workstreams with tasks, gate updates, and acceptance criteria.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project README with quick-start instructions (npm install, npm run dev), available commands (dev, build, lint, typecheck, test, e2e), testing overview, deployment guide, project structure reference, privacy guarantees, and MIT license.

nodes.name: PDR Review — Sign Language Learning Web Platform
nodes.summary: Critique and gap analysis of PDR v1 for the interactive vision-based sign language learning platform. Identifies 12 cross-cutting gaps (personas, success metrics, MVP boundary, accessibility, privacy, Deaf community involvement, content production, model evaluation, fairness, risk register, roadmap, analytics) with prioritized P0/P1/P2 recommendations.

nodes.name: Product Design Requirement (PDR) v2 — Interactive Vision-Based Sign Language Learning Platform
nodes.summary: Refined product design requirement addressing gaps from PDR v1. Includes personas (Hana, Farid, Eli), user stories, MVP/v1/v2 scope columns, functional requirements (onboarding, lesson player, recognition/feedback, progression, accessibility), non-functional requirements (latency, FPS, accuracy, fairness), technical architecture (MediaPipe landmarks, Transformer/ST-GCN, on-device inference), data strategy, privacy/security, WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility, success metrics, and a 6-milestone roadmap.

nodes.name: 03-PDR-summary.html
nodes.summary: One-page HTML visual summary of PDR v2 with dark-themed dashboard layout. Displays product overview, top KPIs (60s time-to-lesson, 300ms latency, 85% accuracy, 95 a11y score), personas, architecture at a glance, scope-by-release table, privacy/accessibility non-negotiables, fairness/community commitments, 6-milestone roadmap, and top changes from PDR v1.

nodes.name: Epics & User Stories — Jira-Ready Backlog
nodes.summary: Jira-ready backlog with 17 epics and 65+ user stories/tasks derived from PDR v2, organized by release (M1 Spike, M2 MVP, M3 Beta, M4 Launch, M5 v2). Includes story points on Fibonacci scale, Given/When/Then acceptance criteria, PDR requirement references, cross-cutting tasks, and definitions of ready/done.

nodes.name: signtutor-redesign-prompt.md
nodes.summary: Complete UI redesign prompt for SignTutor with 'Bioluminescent Studio' dark theme. Defines design tokens (colors, typography, radius, shadows), global components (navbar, cards, badges, buttons), 10 page-by-page redesign specs (home, about, learn, fingerspelling, letter practice, settings, words, practice, setup wizard), animation/motion system, responsive breakpoints, and WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility requirements. Preserves all ML logic, webcam integration, and data schemas.

nodes.name: Dynamic Signs Evaluation Report
nodes.summary: Eval report for dynamic_signs_transformer.onnx — 8-class synthetic landmark sequence classifier with 0.991 held-out accuracy, per-class precision/recall/f1, confusion matrix, fairness slices (NFR-10), and ONNX browser serving instructions via onnxruntime-web.

nodes.name: Dynamic Signs Label Mappings
nodes.summary: Label mappings for dynamic_signs_transformer.onnx — 8 class labels (HELLO, GOODBYE, YES, NO, PLEASE, THANKS, SORRY, HELP) with seq_len=45 and feature_dim=126.

nodes.name: Dynamic Signs Robustness Report
nodes.summary: Robustness stress-test report for dynamic_signs_transformer.onnx — 6 perturbation slices (clean, landmark_jitter, dropped_frames, missing_z, rotated, scale_shift) all within ±5pp gate. All slices pass.

nodes.name: J/Z Classifier Evaluation Report
nodes.summary: Evaluation report for BiLSTM J/Z motion letter classifier — hidden=64, bidirectional, 2 layers, 182K params, (30,126) input, 1.000 test accuracy on 320 samples with perfect precision/recall.

nodes.name: Robustness Gate Demonstration
nodes.summary: Complete NFR-10 robustness gate CI loop walkthrough — detect rotation slice failure (9.4pp), diagnose OOD root cause, apply training-time rotation augmentation fix, re-measure (0.906→0.972), and ship decision with CI YAML example.

nodes.name: J/Z Synthetic Sequence Generator
nodes.summary: Synthetic landmark sequence generator for ASL motion letters J and Z — produces (N, 30, 21, 3) float32 arrays with handshape templates (_j_handshape/_z_handshape), trajectory curves (_j_trajectory/_z_trajectory), noise injection (_apply_noise), and dataset assembly (make_dataset).

nodes.name: Python Dependencies Manifest
nodes.summary: Python dependency manifest for SignTutor ML pipeline — numpy, scikit-learn, torch, onnxruntime, onnx, skl2onnx, opencv-python, mediapipe. Optional FastAPI/uvicorn for API server.

nodes.name: Vercel Deployment Config
nodes.summary: Vercel deployment configuration setting rootDirectory to signtutor for Next.js hosting.

nodes.name: _open_hand
nodes.summary: Build a (21, 3) baseline open-hand layout in palm-local coordinates with all fingers extended, MediaPipe landmark ordering.

nodes.name: _closed_fist
nodes.summary: Derive closed fist handshape from _open_hand — folds tips toward palm via MCP interpolation.

nodes.name: _j_handshape
nodes.summary: J handshape template — pinky extended from closed fist, all other fingers curled.

nodes.name: _z_handshape
nodes.summary: Z handshape template — index extended from closed fist, thumb extended to side, all other fingers curled.

nodes.name: _j_trajectory
nodes.summary: Generate J trajectory path — pinky tip traces downward stroke (0-40% phase) then left hook curve (40-100% phase).

nodes.name: _z_trajectory
nodes.summary: Generate Z trajectory path — index tip traces right stroke (0-33%), diagonal down-left (33-66%), right stroke (66-100%).

nodes.name: _apply_noise
nodes.summary: Apply realistic perturbations per frame — random in-plane rotation, scale variation, and Gaussian landmark jitter.

nodes.name: _generate_j_sequence
nodes.summary: Generate a single J motion sequence (T, 21, 3) — pinky tip along J trajectory, PIP/DIP follower motion, noise injection, wrist drift.

nodes.name: _generate_z_sequence
nodes.summary: Generate a single Z motion sequence (T, 21, 3) — index tip along Z trajectory, PIP/DIP follower motion, noise injection, wrist drift.

nodes.name: make_dataset
nodes.summary: Main entry point — generate synthetic J/Z sequence dataset with n_per_class samples each, returns (X, y, labels) tuple.

nodes.name: PDR v2 — One-Page Summary
nodes.summary: One-page HTML dashboard summarizing PDR v2 with key KPIs (<60s time-to-lesson, <300ms latency, ≥85% accuracy, ≥95 Lighthouse a11y score), personas, architecture at a glance (getUserMedia, MediaPipe, Transformer/ST-GCN, ONNX Runtime), scope-by-release table, roadmap milestones (M0-M5), privacy/accessibility commitments, fairness/community considerations, and top changes from v1.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: SignTutor prototype main page — dual-classifier fingerspelling tutor with ONNX MLP (onnxruntime-web), rule-based fallback, MediaPipe Hands tracking, per-finger interpretability, and structured feedback engine

nodes.name: Dark theme CSS design system
nodes.summary: Dark theme with CSS custom properties (--bg, --panel, --ink, --accent, --ok, --warn, --bad), verdict color coding (match/close/miss), responsive two-column grid layout, per-finger panel, segmented toggle, pill badges, and HUD overlay styles

nodes.name: HTML layout structure
nodes.summary: Sticky header with title and status badges (runtime, model, FPS, privacy), toolbar with language/lesson/classifier controls and start/stop buttons, two-pane row (demo mock signer + webcam section), architecture footer, and activity log

nodes.name: Lesson content definitions
nodes.summary: ASL fingerspelling alphabet targets (24 static letters, excluding J/Z motion letters) with natural-language handshape descriptions for each letter (A through Y)

nodes.name: Synth-collision groups
nodes.summary: Letter groups that the MLP cannot distinguish on finger-pattern alone (mirrors SYNTH_COLLISIONS in ml/synth_landmarks.py): A/E/M/N/S/T, D/G/L/Q, H/U/V, K/P, C/O; used to soften verdicts to 'close' for same-group predictions

nodes.name: Feature normalization pipeline
nodes.summary: normalize() function — byte-equivalent mirror of ml/features.py: translates landmarks to wrist-origin, scales by wrist-to-middle-MCP xy distance, outputs Float32Array(63); enforces JS-Python parity contract for ONNX inference

nodes.name: ONNX MLP inference engine
nodes.summary: Loads fingerspell_mlp.onnx via onnxruntime-web, resolves labels from models/labels.json, runs forward pass with softmax output, returns top-3 ranked predictions with probabilities; falls back to rule-based if model or runtime unavailable

nodes.name: Startup parity self-test
nodes.summary: PDR-grade contract check: loads canonical landmark frames from models/parity_samples.json, runs JS normalize() and compares features to Python reference (~1e-4 tolerance), then runs ONNX model and compares top-1 labels to ground truth; logs pass/fail per letter

nodes.name: Rule-based classifier
nodes.summary: Deterministic classifier using finger-extension heuristics: dist-to-wrist ratio per finger determines extended/curled state, then matches against target letter's PATTERNS entry; mirrors PATTERNS in ml/synth_landmarks.py; used as fallback and for per-finger tips in ML mode

nodes.name: Finger extension pattern definitions
nodes.summary: 24 letter patterns (A-Y) defining which fingers are extended vs curled for each ASL fingerspelling letter; mirrors PATTERNS dict in ml/synth_landmarks.py; each entry has thumb/index/middle/ring/pinky boolean states

nodes.name: MediaPipe hand tracking integration
nodes.summary: Camera getUserMedia → MediaPipe Hands (maxNumHands:1, modelComplexity:1) → onResults callback → landmark drawing via drawConnectors/drawLandmarks → movement buffer → classifyAndScore dispatch; latency HUD and FPS counter

nodes.name: Structured feedback engine
nodes.summary: Per-frame scoring across four components (handshape via ML probability, orientation via cross-product, location via y-coordinate, movement via wrist displacement), aggregate confidence gating, verdict selection (match/close/miss), and actionable tip generation using weakest-component and per-finger rule analysis

nodes.name: Per-finger interpretability panel
nodes.summary: Visual five-finger grid showing extended/curled state for each finger compared to target pattern; green match / red miss indicators with text feedback ('should extend', 'should curl', 'extend ✓', 'curl ✓'); drives actionable tips from rule layer

nodes.name: External model files referenced
nodes.summary: Three external files loaded at runtime: models/fingerspell_mlp.onnx (sklearn MLP exported to ONNX), models/labels.json (class label array), models/parity_samples.json (canonical landmark frames with expected outputs for self-test)

observations: Word-level label config with 8 dynamic sign language labels
Labels: HELLO, GOODBYE, YES, NO, PLEASE, THANKS, SORRY, HELP
seq_len=45 (sequence length for model input)
feature_dim=126 (feature dimension per time step)

Labels: A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, K, L, M, N, O, P, Q, R, S, T, U, V, W, X, Y
Defines synth_collisions grouping visually similar letters for synthetic data augmentation
Collision groups: [A,E,M,N,S,T], [D,G,L,Q], [H,U,V], [K,P], [C,O]

observations: Parity test samples for model validation, one entry per letter
Contains 5 entries: B, F, I, L, Y
Each entry has 21 hand landmarks with xyz coordinates
Each entry includes expected_normalized_first6 (normalized landmark comparison values)
Each entry includes expected_normalized_l2 (L2 norm of normalized landmarks)

observations: Design system document for SignTutor v2
Theme: 'Bioluminescent Studio' — dark, focused, deep-sea research lab aesthetic
Background: near-black #080c14 with blue undertone, surfaces #0f1420, elevated #161d2e
Primary accent: bioluminescent teal #00e5b0; secondary: electric blue #4f8cff
Typography: Syne (headings), DM Sans (body), JetBrains Mono (labels/stats)
Radius tokens: sm=8px, md=14px, lg=20px, xl=28px
Motion: ease-out-quart/expo curves, no bounce/elastic, respects prefers-reduced-motion
Component patterns: cards with hover glow, pill badges, teal/ghost/secondary buttons
nodes.summary: No summary available
nodes.name: DESIGN.md

observations: Implementation status tracking for SignTutor v2
Completed: webcam start button fixed (external script loader in src/lib/loadExternals.ts)
Completed: MediaPipe Holistic integration (hands+face+pose in single pass)
Completed: CalibrationLayer with temperature scaling (src/lib/calibration.ts)
Completed: Structured feedback panel (handshape, orientation, location, movement, expression)
Completed: Privacy fetch interceptor (src/lib/privacyGate.ts, PrivacyGateWrapper component)
Completed: TypeScript check passes, 31/31 tests pass
Remaining W1: real data foundation (WLASL/ChicagoFS sources, signer-disjoint split, license audit)
Remaining W2: J/Z motion letters (dynamic Transformer inference, 45-frame buffer to ONNX)
Remaining W3: 200 dynamic signs (vocabulary truncated at 36, Transformer .onnx needed)
Remaining W4: NMM scoring behind flag (MLP heads for brow/mouth not implemented)
Remaining W5: on-device personalization adapter (confirmation UI not built)
Remaining W6: confidence calibration (reliability diagram not shown in lessons)
Remaining W7: multi-language beta (ASL/BSL/ArSL bundles defined, lazy-loading not wired)
Remaining W8: continuous-sign research preview (route exists, no model running)
CI Gates: signer-disjoint check, license grep gate, ECE gate, privacy egress gate, per-language CI
nodes.summary: No summary available
nodes.name: IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md

observations: Product context document for SignTutor
Users: Deaf/HoH learning ASL, hearing people, educators, interpreters, privacy-conscious users
Brand: SignTutor, tagline 'Learn ASL with real-time, on-device feedback'
Tone: Professional, warm, intelligent, accessible
Product: browser-based ASL learning with real-time webcam feedback via ML
Privacy-first: no video uploads, no accounts, no cloud processing
Strategic principle 1: On-device by default — all ML runs locally
Strategic principle 2: Accessibility first — WCAG 2.2 AA, keyboard nav, screen readers
Strategic principle 3: Progressive learning — FSRS spaced repetition, adaptive difficulty
Strategic principle 4: Hands are the hero — celebrates human hand communication

nodes.summary: No summary available
nodes.name: tsconfig.json
Impeccable Asset Producer
nodes.summary: Agent instruction for producing clean reusable raster assets from approved mock references without redesigning direction. Defines input/output contracts, workflow buckets (produce/direct/semantic), prompt patterns, and QA statuses.

nodes.name: cleanup-deprecated.mjs
nodes.summary: Cleans up deprecated Impeccable skill files, symlinks, and skills-lock.json entries from previous versions. Scans harness directories (.claude, .cursor, .agents, etc.) for renamed/merged skills and removes them. Safe to run repeatedly.

nodes.name: command-metadata.json
nodes.summary: JSON metadata defining all Impeccable sub-commands (craft, teach, document, extract, live, adapt, animate, audit, bolder, etc.) with descriptions and argument hints for the skill pin system.

nodes.name: critique-storage.mjs
nodes.summary: Critique persistence helper. Writes per-target snapshots to .impeccable/critique/ with YAML frontmatter. Provides CLI commands: slug, write, latest, trend. Slug derived mechanically from resolved artifact path/URL.

nodes.name: design-parser.mjs
nodes.summary: Parses DESIGN.md (Stitch-spec format) into structured JSON model. Handles YAML frontmatter, six canonical H2 sections (Overview, Colors, Typography, Elevation, Components, Do's and Don'ts), color extraction, font roles, shadows, and component variants. Includes coverage assessment.

nodes.name: detect-csp.mjs
nodes.summary: Scans project tree for Content-Security-Policy signals and classifies the CSP shape (append-arrays, append-string, middleware, meta-tag) for patch template selection. Used at first-time live.mjs setup. Grep-based, no network or JS eval.

nodes.name: detect.mjs
nodes.summary: Entry point that locates and loads the bundled detect-antipatterns detector script. Resolves detector from multiple candidate paths, dynamically imports it, and runs detectCli().

nodes.name: impeccable-paths.mjs
nodes.summary: Shared path resolution utilities for the Impeccable skill. Provides functions for .impeccable directory paths, design sidecar, live config, server info, sessions, annotations, and critique storage paths. Handles legacy path fallbacks.

nodes.name: is-generated.mjs
nodes.summary: Determines whether a file is generated (unsafe to edit) or source (safe to persist). Uses git check-ignore and file-header markers (@generated, DO NOT EDIT, AUTO-GENERATED) within first 300 bytes.

nodes.name: live-accept.mjs
nodes.summary: CLI helper for deterministic accept/discard of live variant sessions. Handles discarding (removes wrapper, restores original) and accepting (replaces wrapper with chosen variant, preserves colocated style blocks for carbonization). Handles JSX/HTML syntax differences.

nodes.name: live-browser-session.js
nodes.summary: Browser-side durable session helpers for Impeccable live mode. Provides localStorage-backed session persistence with checkpoint revision tracking, handled-state markers, and scroll position save/restore. Attached to window.__IMPECCABLE_LIVE_SESSION__.

nodes.name: live-browser.js
nodes.summary: Main browser-side script for Impeccable Live Variant Mode (4860 lines). Full UI system: floating element picker bar with three modes (configure/generating/cycling), annotation overlay with pins and strokes, WebGL shader overlay during generation, design system panel, variant cycling, scroll lock, SSE communication, and session resume after HMR/reload.

nodes.name: live-complete.mjs
nodes.summary: Canonical durable completion acknowledgement for Impeccable live sessions. Appends final completion events (complete, discarded, agent_error) to the session journal. Tries server notification first, falls back to local store.

nodes.name: live-completion.mjs
nodes.summary: Deterministic completion type logic for accept results. Maps event type + accept result to completion status (discarded, complete, agent_done, error). Generates completion ack objects with carbonize requirements.

nodes.name: live-inject.mjs
nodes.summary: CLI helper to insert/remove the live variant mode script tag in project HTML entry points. Reads configuration from .impeccable/live/config.json. Handles glob file resolution, CSP meta-tag patching for localhost, and indentation-preserving tag removal.

nodes.name: live-poll.mjs
nodes.summary: CLI client for the live variant mode poll/reply protocol. Blocks until a browser event arrives (long-poll with configurable timeout), auto-handles accept/discard via live-accept.mjs, and routes completion acknowledgements. Supports --reply mode for agent-driven replies.

nodes.name: live-resume.mjs
nodes.summary: Recovers the next agent action from the durable live-session journal. Reads session store, determines pending events, and outputs the appropriate next action (poll, carbonize cleanup, or complete).

nodes.name: live-server.mjs
nodes.summary: Self-contained live variant mode HTTP server (zero dependencies). Serves browser scripts, handles SSE for server-to-browser push, HTTP POST for browser-to-server events, long-poll for agent communication, annotation upload, design system API, and session lifecycle management. Supports background mode and port auto-detection.

nodes.name: live-session-store.mjs
nodes.summary: Durable session store for Impeccable live mode. Manages event journals (.jsonl files) and snapshot caching. Provides appendEvent, getSnapshot, and listActiveSessions. Handles state machine transitions (generate_requested, variants_ready, carbonize_required, accept_requested, etc.).

nodes.name: live-status.mjs
nodes.summary: Prints durable recovery status for Impeccable live sessions. Fetches server status and lists active sessions from the session store. Provides recovery hints based on server availability.

nodes.name: live-wrap.mjs
nodes.summary: CLI helper to find an element in source and wrap it in a variant container. Searches project files by ID, classes, tag, or text query. Handles HTML vs JSX syntax, generates wrapper with marker comments, and calculates insert lines for variant placement.

nodes.name: live.mjs
nodes.summary: Orchestration entry point for live variant mode. Checks config, starts/reuses live server, injects browser script tag, loads PRODUCT.md/DESIGN.md context, scans for config drift, and emits a single JSON blob with everything the agent needs to enter the poll loop.

nodes.name: load-context.mjs
nodes.summary: Shared context loader for Impeccable commands. Resolves PRODUCT.md and DESIGN.md files through directory lookup chain (env var, cwd, .agents/context/, docs/). Handles legacy .impeccable.md to PRODUCT.md auto-migration. Exports both CLI and programmatic API.

nodes.name: modern-screenshot.umd.js
nodes.summary: Vendored UMD build of the modern-screenshot library. Lazy-loaded by live-browser.js at runtime for DOM-to-image capture. Exposes window.modernScreenshot.domToBlob() with font embedding, CSS cloning, and DPI support.

nodes.name: pin.mjs
nodes.summary: Pin/unpin Impeccable sub-commands as standalone skill shortcuts across all harness directories. Creates lightweight SKILL.md files that redirect to /impeccable <command>. Uses PIN_MARKER for safe unpinning. Reads command metadata from command-metadata.json.

nodes.name: findProjectRoot
nodes.summary: Walks up from startDir to find project root by checking for package.json, .git, or skills-lock.json.

nodes.name: loadLock
nodes.summary: Loads skills-lock.json from project root, returns null if missing or unreadable.

nodes.name: isImpeccableSkill
nodes.summary: Checks whether a skill directory belongs to Impeccable using lock file source, SKILL.md content match, or per-skill fingerprint.

nodes.name: buildTargetNames
nodes.summary: Builds full list of deprecated names including i-prefixed variants.

nodes.name: findSkillsDirs
nodes.summary: Finds all harness skills directories that exist on disk.

nodes.name: removeDeprecatedSkills
nodes.summary: Removes deprecated skill directories/symlinks from all harness dirs using authoritative lock file signals.

nodes.name: cleanSkillsLock
nodes.summary: Removes deprecated entries from skills-lock.json where source is pbakaus/impeccable.

nodes.name: cleanup
nodes.summary: Run full cleanup: deletes deprecated directories first, then strips lock entries. Returns summary object.

nodes.name: slugFromTarget
nodes.summary: Mechanically derives a stable slug from a resolved target (file path or URL). Project-relative for consistency across clones.

nodes.name: nowFilenameStamp
nodes.summary: Generates a filename-safe UTC ISO timestamp with hyphens instead of colons.

nodes.name: writeSnapshot
nodes.summary: Writes a critique snapshot to .impeccable/critique/ with YAML frontmatter and body content.

nodes.name: readLatestSnapshot
nodes.summary: Returns the most recent snapshot for a slug, or null.

nodes.name: readTrend
nodes.summary: Returns last N snapshots' frontmatter for trend display.

nodes.name: parseDesignMd
nodes.summary: Main entry: parses DESIGN.md markdown into structured JSON model with frontmatter and six canonical sections.

nodes.name: assessCoverage
nodes.summary: Assesses coverage of all six DESIGN.md sections, returns structured report.

nodes.name: detectCsp
nodes.summary: Scans project for CSP signals and classifies shape (append-arrays, append-string, middleware, meta-tag, or null).

nodes.name: getImpeccableDir
nodes.summary: Returns path to .impeccable directory.

nodes.name: getDesignSidecarPath
nodes.summary: Returns path to .impeccable/design.json.

nodes.name: resolveDesignSidecarPath
nodes.summary: Resolves the first existing design sidecar from candidate paths.

nodes.name: getLiveDir
nodes.summary: Returns path to .impeccable/live/ directory.

nodes.name: getLiveConfigPath
nodes.summary: Returns path to .impeccable/live/config.json.

nodes.name: resolveLiveConfigPath
nodes.summary: Resolves live config path from env var, primary location, or legacy fallback.

nodes.name: getLiveServerPath
nodes.summary: Returns path to .impeccable/live/server.json.

nodes.name: readLiveServerInfo
nodes.summary: Reads live server info from server.json or legacy .impeccable-live.json.

nodes.name: writeLiveServerInfo
nodes.summary: Writes live server info to server.json.

nodes.name: removeLiveServerInfo
nodes.summary: Removes live server info files.

nodes.name: getLiveSessionsDir
nodes.summary: Returns path to .impeccable/live/sessions/.

nodes.name: getCritiqueDir
nodes.summary: Returns path to .impeccable/critique/.

nodes.name: isGeneratedFile
nodes.summary: Determines if a file is generated via git check-ignore or header markers.

nodes.name: acceptCli
nodes.summary: CLI entry for accept/discard. Parses args, finds session file, dispatches to handleDiscard or handleAccept.

nodes.name: findMarkerBlock
nodes.summary: Finds start/end marker line indices for a session in file lines.

nodes.name: extractOriginal
nodes.summary: Extracts original element content from variant wrapper.

nodes.name: extractVariant
nodes.summary: Extracts a specific variant's inner content.

nodes.name: extractCss
nodes.summary: Extracts colocated style block content from variant wrapper.

nodes.name: deindentContent
nodes.summary: De-indents content back to base indent level.

nodes.name: detectCommentSyntax
nodes.summary: Detects HTML vs JSX comment syntax from file extension.

nodes.name: completionTypeForAcceptResult
nodes.summary: Maps event type + accept result to completion status string.

nodes.name: completionAckForAcceptResult
nodes.summary: Generates completion acknowledgement object with carbonize requirements.

nodes.name: injectCli
nodes.summary: CLI entry for inject/remove/check modes. Handles port-based insertion and tag removal across resolved files.

nodes.name: resolveFiles
nodes.summary: Expands config files (literal + glob) into existing file paths, applying exclusion filters.

nodes.name: patchCspMeta
nodes.summary: Patches CSP meta tags to allow localhost connections, storing original values for revert.

nodes.name: revertCspMeta
nodes.summary: Restores CSP meta tags to their original content values using stored marker attribute.

nodes.name: buildPollReplyPayload
nodes.summary: Builds a poll reply payload with token, id, type, message, file, and data.

nodes.name: pollCli
nodes.summary: Main poll CLI: blocks for browser events, auto-handles accept/discard, loops with configurable timeout.

nodes.name: createLiveSessionStore
nodes.summary: Factory for durable session store with appendEvent, getSnapshot, and listActiveSessions methods. Manages journal files and snapshot cache.

nodes.name: wrapCli
nodes.summary: Main CLI entry: finds element in source, wraps it with variant container, handles ambiguity detection via text content matching.

nodes.name: buildSearchQueries
nodes.summary: Builds search query strings in priority order (ID, classes, tag+class, raw query).

nodes.name: findElement
nodes.summary: Finds element in file lines by matching query and resolving tag opener/closer.

nodes.name: findClosingLine
nodes.summary: Finds matching closing tag line by counting tag nesting depth.

nodes.name: detectCommentSyntax
nodes.summary: Detects HTML vs JSX comment syntax from file extension.

nodes.name: resolveContextDir
nodes.summary: Resolves the directory containing PRODUCT.md/DESIGN.md via env var, cwd check, or fallback subdirs.

nodes.name: loadContext
nodes.summary: Loads PRODUCT.md and DESIGN.md content, handles legacy .impeccable.md migration, returns structured context object.

nodes.name: findOpenPort
nodes.summary: Finds an available TCP port starting from given port number.

nodes.name: createLiveBrowserSessionState
nodes.summary: Factory for browser-side session state with localStorage persistence, checkpoint revisions, and scroll tracking.

nodes.name: synth_landmarks.py
nodes.summary: Synthetic landmark generator for ASL fingerspelling — produces 21 MediaPipe hand landmarks for 24 static letters (A-Y, excluding J/Z) using finger extension/curling patterns; models each letter as per-finger boolean pattern with positional noise and palm variation for data augmentation

nodes.name: _polar
nodes.summary: Convert angle(deg)+length to (x,y) vector via sin/cos; used for finger joint positioning

nodes.name: _synthesize_one
nodes.summary: Generate a single (21,3) landmark frame for a given letter using its finger pattern, with randomized wrist position, palm rotation/scale, per-joint noise, and curling for non-extended fingers

nodes.name: make_dataset
nodes.summary: Generate full synthetic landmark dataset: n_per_class samples per letter, returns (N,21,3) float32 array + int64 labels + letter list; supports subset selection

nodes.name: synth_sequences.py
nodes.summary: Synthetic sign-language sequence generator — produces (T,21,3) landmark tensors (T=45 frames) for 8 isolated signs (HELLO, GOODBYE, YES, NO, PLEASE, THANKS, SORRY, HELP); each sign has templated wrist trajectory + finger shape animation; mirrors MediaPipe Hands output shape

nodes.name: _open_hand
nodes.summary: Build a (21,3) baseline open-hand landmark template in palm-local coordinates with all fingers extended

nodes.name: _closed_fist
nodes.summary: Build a closed-fist (21,3) landmark template by folding finger tips toward palm from the open-hand base

nodes.name: _index_middle_extended
nodes.summary: Build a two-finger V-shape (21,3) template from closed fist by extending index and middle finger tips

nodes.name: _flat_hand
nodes.summary: Alias for _open_hand; returns flat extended hand template

nodes.name: _interp
nodes.summary: Linear interpolation between two values a and b by alpha factor

nodes.name: _wrist_path
nodes.summary: Return wrist (x,y,z) trajectory per frame for each sign name — HELLO sweeps outward, GOODBYE waves, YES bobs vertically, NO stays still, PLEASE/SORRY circle, THANKS moves outward, HELP lifts up

nodes.name: _hand_shape_for_frame
nodes.summary: Return palm-local (21,3) landmarks for a sign at animation fraction alpha (0-1); interpolates between flat fist, closed fist, or V-shape depending on sign

nodes.name: _synthesize_one
nodes.summary: Generate a single (T,21,3) sequence for a named sign: combines wrist trajectory + finger shape animation with palm scale jitter, rotation, per-landmark noise, and camera jitter

nodes.name: make_dataset
nodes.summary: Generate full synthetic sequence dataset: n_per_class per sign, returns (N,T,21,3) float32 array + int64 labels + label names list

nodes.name: train_export_v2.py
nodes.summary: Improved MLP training pipeline for ASL fingerspelling — 68-dim features (63 landmarks + 5 geometry features for R/U/V distinction), mirror augmentation (2x), jitter augmentation for hard classes, sample weighting (2.25x for collision groups), 256→128→64 architecture with early stopping; outputs fingerspell_mlp_v2.onnx

nodes.name: compute_geometry_features
nodes.summary: Compute 5 geometry features from (21,3) landmarks: index-middle tip distance, MCP distance, cosine angle, cross product sign, tip/MCP ratio; explicitly captures finger relationships to distinguish R/U/V letters

nodes.name: expand_features_with_geometry
nodes.summary: Expand (N,21,3) raw landmarks to (N,68) feature vectors: 63 normalized landmarks via features.normalize + 5 geometry features via compute_geometry_features

nodes.name: mirror_augment
nodes.summary: Mirror-augment landmark dataset by negating x-coordinates, doubling dataset size for left/right hand invariance

nodes.name: jitter_augment
nodes.summary: Apply jitter augmentation to hard classes (R,U,V,M,N,O): each hard sample gets n_jitters additional versions with random rotation (up to 0.42 rad) and Gaussian noise (std 0.018)

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Full training pipeline: generate synthetic landmarks → mirror augment → jitter augment → expand features → train/test split → sample weighting → train MLPClassifier(256,128,64) → eval with collision-aware accuracy → export to ONNX → write labels_v2.json and eval_report_v2.md

nodes.name: train_export.py
nodes.summary: Original MLP training pipeline for ASL fingerspelling — generates synthetic landmarks, normalizes to 63-dim features, trains MLPClassifier(128,64) with early stopping, exports to fingerspell_mlp.onnx; includes collision-aware accuracy reporting and fairness slices

nodes.name: train_jz_classifier.py
nodes.summary: PyTorch BiLSTM classifier for ASL motion letters J and Z — trains on synthetic landmark sequences, 2-layer bidirectional LSTM (hidden=64, dropout=0.2), exports to jz_classifier.onnx for browser inference

nodes.name: JZClassifier
nodes.summary: Small 2-layer bidirectional LSTM classifier for J/Z motion letters: input_proj(L,126→64) → BiLSTM(64,2 layers) → classifier(128→64 ReLU → 64→2); uses last hidden state for classification

nodes.name: JZClassifierForExport
nodes.summary: ONNX export wrapper around JZClassifier — delegates forward() to inner model, used for clean ONNX graph export with logits output

nodes.name: train_transformer_v2.py
nodes.summary: Scaled Transformer encoder (d_model=128, 4 layers, 8 heads, FFN=256) for 200-class dynamic isolated-sign recognition; exports to dynamic_transformer_v2.onnx (~1.4 MB); exposes embedding output for W5 PersonalAdapter; supports synthetic and real data sources

nodes.name: DynamicTransformerV2
nodes.summary: Scaled Transformer encoder for dynamic sign recognition: input_proj(126→128) + learned pos_emb → 4-layer Pre-LN TransformerEncoder with GELU, 8 heads, FFN=256 → AdaptiveAvgPool1D → classifier; optionally exports embedding for personalization adapter

nodes.name: train_transformer.py
nodes.summary: Tiny Transformer encoder (~25k params) for dynamic isolated-sign recognition — 4 heads, 2 layers, d_model=64, d_in=126; trained on 8 synthetic sign classes; exports to dynamic_signs_transformer.onnx; includes optional rotation augmentation and ONNX-PyTorch parity check

nodes.name: rotate_clip
nodes.summary: Rotate a (T,21,3) landmark clip in the image plane around the first-frame wrist by a given angle in degrees

nodes.name: cm_md
nodes.summary: Generate a markdown confusion matrix table from a sklearn confusion matrix and label list

nodes.name: SignTransformer
nodes.summary: Tiny Transformer encoder: input_proj(126→64) + learned pos_emb → 2-layer TransformerEncoder(4 heads, GELU, d_model=64) → LayerNorm → mean-pool → linear head to n_classes; ~25k params, designed for browser deployment

nodes.name: dynamic_signs_transformer.onnx
nodes.summary: ONNX model file — tiny Transformer encoder for dynamic isolated-sign recognition, trained by ml/train_transformer.py, ~25k params, loaded by onnxruntime-web in browser

nodes.name: fingerspell_mlp.onnx
nodes.summary: ONNX model file — sklearn MLP classifier for ASL fingerspelling, trained by ml/train_export.py, loaded by onnxruntime-web in the browser prototype

nodes.name: screenshot-test.js
nodes.summary: Playwright script that takes a single full-page screenshot of the letter-a page at localhost:3000/learn/asl/fingerspelling/a

nodes.name: screenshot.js
nodes.summary: Playwright script that captures full-page screenshots of 10 app pages (homepage, about, learn, fingerspelling, letter-a/b, settings, words, practice, setup) at 1920x1080

nodes.name: letter-a-test.png
nodes.summary: Full-page screenshot of letter A lesson page taken by screenshot-test.js Playwright script

nodes.name: curriculum-v2.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest test suite for curriculum-v2 module — verifies 24 static letters, 2 motion letters (J/Z), isMotionLetter, LETTER_DESCRIPTIONS, MOTION_DESCRIPTIONS, getStaticLetter, getMotionLetter, and getDynamicSign behaviors

nodes.name: lib.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest test suite for normalize lib module — tests normalize() output shape, wrist-centering, palm-width scaling; fingerStates returns all 5 fingers; inSameGroup collision group matching; classifyRule ranked results; palmFacingCamera; locationLabel; normalizeSequence shape and anchoring; COLLISION_GROUPS and PATTERNS coverage

nodes.name: parity.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest parity test — loads parity_samples.json, runs JS normalize() against Python reference, asserts first-6 drift < 1e-3 and L2 drift < 1e-3 per letter

nodes.name: privacy.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest privacy gate test — asserts that no fetch/XHR request body contains landmark tensors, embeddings, or adapter weights during a practice session, body length < 1024 bytes

nodes.name: check_ece.py
nodes.summary: CI script that computes Expected Calibration Error (ECE) from model logits and labels using numpy binning, with a configurable threshold check to gate model quality.

nodes.name: compute_ece
nodes.summary: Computes Expected Calibration Error by binning model confidence scores (max prob per sample) into n_bins intervals and comparing bin accuracy to bin confidence, returning a weighted absolute difference.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point loading probs.npy and labels.npy, computing ECE, and returning exit code 1 if ECE exceeds the threshold.

nodes.name: check_licenses.py
nodes.summary: CI script that verifies every dataset directory under ml/data/ has a corresponding entry in ml/data/LICENSES.md, failing if any directory lacks documentation.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Reads LICENSES.md, iterates data directories, and collects names missing from the license file, returning exit code 1 if any are undocumented.

nodes.name: check_signer_disjoint.py
nodes.summary: CI script that checks train and test signer sets are disjoint (no signer leakage between splits), loading signer lists from JSON metadata files.

nodes.name: load_signers
nodes.summary: Loads a set of signer names from a JSON file's 'signers' key.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point loading train and test signer sets, computing overlap, and returning exit code 1 if any signer appears in both sets.

nodes.name: dev-err.log
nodes.summary: Next.js development error log showing a Turbopack workspace root detection warning (multiple lockfiles) and a port conflict error — another dev server already running on localhost:3000 (PID 22360).

nodes.name: dev.log
nodes.summary: Next.js 16.2.6 (Turbopack) dev server startup log — ready on localhost:3002 (network 192.168.1.36:3002) in 433ms.

nodes.name: dynamic-lesson.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright E2E test for the dynamic lesson player (/learn/asl/words/:slug) verifying page title, target sign display, Start/Record/Stop controls, 45-frame buffer UI, privacy badge, and sign-to-sign navigation routing.

nodes.name: static-lesson.spec.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright E2E test for the static fingerspelling lesson player verifying page title, target letter display, Start/Stop camera controls, per-finger handshape match panel, privacy badge, and letter-to-letter navigation.

nodes.name: eslint.config.mjs
nodes.summary: ESLint flat config using eslint-config-next core-web-vitals and TypeScript presets, with global ignores for .next, out, build, e2e, and Playwright config files, plus custom no-unused-vars rule with underscore prefix convention.

nodes.name: next.config.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js configuration with Content-Security-Policy headers for /learn/* and /practice routes allowing MediaPipe CDN scripts, onnxruntime-web wasm, Wikimedia images, and blob media; includes remote image pattern for upload.wikimedia.org.

nodes.name: playwright.config.ts
nodes.summary: Playwright E2E configuration targeting Desktop Chrome, with fullyParallel, CI-aware retries/forbidOnly, single worker, GitHub reporter in CI, video+trace on first retry, auto-starting Next.js dev server for local runs.

nodes.name: postcss.config.mjs
nodes.summary: PostCSS configuration enabling the @tailwindcss/postcss plugin for Tailwind CSS v4 processing.

nodes.name: fingerspell_mlp_v2.onnx
nodes.summary: ONNX MLP v2 model for fingerspelling recognition, served statically for client-side inference.

nodes.name: fingerspell_mlp.onnx
nodes.summary: ONNX MLP model for fingerspelling recognition, smaller than the v2 variant, served statically for client-side inference.

nodes.name: jz_classifier.onnx
nodes.summary: ONNX classifier model (JZ classifier), served statically for client-side ONNX Runtime inference.

nodes.name: favicon.ico

nodes.name: globals.css

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: ASL fingerspelling letter selection page — client component with filter tabs (all/static/motion), grid of letter cards linking to individual letter practice pages, using curriculum data from @/lib/curriculum

nodes.name: [letter]/page.tsx
nodes.summary: Individual ASL letter practice page — client component with webcam integration, MediaPipe hand landmark tracking, rule-based classification (LocationLabel, PalmFacingCamera, RecentMovement, FingerStates), ONNX model inference, real-time per-finger feedback display, letter reference images, and comprehensive UI for practicing a single letter

nodes.name: SettingsPage
nodes.summary: User settings page for hand preference, mirror mode, and advanced beta features

nodes.name: DynamicSignPage
nodes.summary: Dynamic sign practice page with webcam, MediaPipe hand tracking, ONNX Transformer inference, and real-time feedback

nodes.name: WordsPage
nodes.summary: Vocabulary browser with search, category filtering, and sign cards showing ML-enabled status

nodes.name: LearnPage
nodes.summary: Curriculum hub showing learning modules, progress bar, and navigation to fingerspelling, vocabulary, and spaced repetition

nodes.name: Home
nodes.summary: Landing page with hero section, feature cards, tech badges, and footer navigation

nodes.name: PracticePage
nodes.summary: Spaced repetition review page with FSRS-powered queue, progress stats, and sign status grid

nodes.name: PrivacyPage
nodes.summary: Privacy policy page detailing on-device processing, local storage, network requests, and camera access

nodes.name: ContinuousResearchPage
nodes.summary: Research preview server component for continuous sign language recognition

nodes.name: ResearchClient
nodes.summary: Research preview client component with consent gate, reading timer, and CTC decoder placeholder for continuous SLR

nodes.name: SetupPage
nodes.summary: 4-step setup wizard: language selection, camera permission, lighting check, framing and handedness

nodes.name: Header
nodes.summary: Sticky header with navigation links, mobile hamburger menu, and on-device privacy badge

nodes.name: PrivacyGateWrapper
nodes.summary: Privacy gate interceptor installer component — renders null, installs fetch/XHR interceptors on mount

nodes.name: ReliabilityDiagram
nodes.summary: SVG reliability diagram showing model calibration with accuracy vs confidence bars and ECE score

nodes.name: CalibrationLayer
nodes.summary: Temperature scaling calibration with CalibrationLayer class, ECE computation, and temperature fitting via binary search

nodes.name: CurriculumV2
nodes.summary: v2 curriculum extension with 24 static letters, J/Z motion letters, 8 dynamic signs, reference images, descriptions, and curriculum item builder

nodes.name: Curriculum
nodes.summary: Core curriculum data: 26 static letters, J/Z motion letters, 8 dynamic signs, sign descriptions, curriculum item builder, and helpers

nodes.name: LanguageBundles
nodes.summary: Multi-language ONNX bundle management for ASL, BSL, and ArSL with model paths and metadata

nodes.name: LoadExternals
nodes.summary: Dynamic script loader for MediaPipe Hands and ONNX Runtime Web from CDN with caching

nodes.name: HolisticRunner
nodes.summary: MediaPipe Holistic runner for v2 that returns hands + face + pose landmarks simultaneously, with NMM feature extraction

nodes.name: Normalize
nodes.summary: Hand landmark normalization, finger extension detection, rule-based letter classification, collision groups, and movement analysis

nodes.name: PersonalAdapter
nodes.summary: On-device personalization layer with frozen ONNX backbone + small low-rank adapter trained in-browser via SGD

nodes.name: PrivacyGate
nodes.summary: Privacy gate interceptors for fetch() and XMLHttpRequest to prevent landmark/embedding/adapter data exfiltration

nodes.name: SeqFeatures
nodes.summary: Sequence normalization for dynamic sign recognition: wrist-centered, scale-invariant with velocity features and fixed-length padding

nodes.name: Storage
nodes.summary: localStorage wrapper for preferences, progress tracking, FSRS scheduling, and review queue computation

nodes.name: Vocabulary200
nodes.summary: 200 high-frequency ASL signs with structured reference data: gloss, handshape, location, movement, orientation, NMM, and ASL-LEX rankings

nodes.name: SettingsPage
nodes.summary: Default export — renders settings page with toggle switches for mirror, handedness, and placeholder advanced beta features

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Understand SignTutor's purpose as a browser-based, vision-driven sign language learning platform. Review the README for quick-start, commands, and structure; package.json for dependencies (Next.js 16, React 19, onnxruntime-web); and PRODUCT.md for strategic principles including on-device ML and WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility.

tour.title: Architecture & Design
tour.description: Explore the high-level architecture decisions: the BUILD_PROMPT defines 8 architectural invariants (on-device privacy, confidence-gated verdict, per-finger panel, JS↔Python parity). The refined PDR v2 specifies personas, user stories, ML pipeline (MediaPipe→Transformer/ST-GCN→ONNX), and a 6-milestone roadmap. DESIGN.md documents the 'Bioluminescent Studio' dark theme with design tokens.

tour.title: Entry Point & Routing
tour.description: Trace the Next.js App Router entry: page.tsx is the landing/home page, globals.css defines global Tailwind styles, learn/page.tsx is the learning hub hub, and fingerspelling pages handle per-letter lessons. Together they form the user-facing navigation structure.

tour.title: Component Tree
tour.description: Examine the shared React components: Header renders navigation and runtime status; PrivacyGateWrapper enforces the on-device privacy promise with a fetch interceptor; ReliabilityDiagram displays model confidence calibration. These are reused across pages for consistent UX.

tour.title: Core Library — Normalization & Curriculum
tour.description: Dive into the central logic: normalize.ts provides the JS↔Python byte-equivalent landmark normalization translating wrist-origin MediaPipe landmarks into ONNX-ready feature vectors. curriculum-v2.ts implements FSRS-based spaced repetition for adaptive lesson sequencing. storage.ts handles local persistence with privacy guarantees.

tour.title: ML Inference Pipeline
tour.description: Understand the browser-side ML runtime: holisticRunner.ts integrates MediaPipe Holistic (hands+face+pose) for single-pass landmark extraction; loadExternals.ts manages dynamic script loading for MediaPipe and ONNX runtime; calibration.ts applies temperature scaling for calibrated confidence scores and the reliability diagram.

tour.title: ML Training & Synthetic Data
tour.description: Explore the Python ML pipeline: synth_landmarks.py generates synthetic 21-point MediaPipe landmarks for 24 static ASL letters; synth_sequences.py produces 45-frame landmark sequences for 8 dynamic signs; train_export_v2.py trains and exports an MLP to ONNX with mirror/jitter augmentation and collision-aware accuracy.

tour.title: Testing & CI Quality Gates
tour.description: Review the testing strategy: parity.test.ts validates JS↔Python landmark normalization byte-equivalence; static-lesson.spec.ts and dynamic-lesson.spec.ts are Playwright E2E tests; vitest and playwright configs define the test runners. CI gates (check_ece.py, check_licenses.py, check_signer_disjoint.py) enforce model calibration error, license compliance, and signer-disjoint validation.
